name: comet-standard
title: COMET Standard
description: Frictionless Table Schema for the COMET comics metadata standard. Generated from standard/fields/*.yaml. Validates COMET CSV files by column, type, required status, and controlled-vocabulary (picklist) values.

fields.name: UPC
fields.title: UPC
fields.description: The UPC field provides the 12-digit Universal Product Code and 5-digit supplements for all items which carry a UPC. Example 75960608857704611  [Required for comics]

fields.name: ISBN
fields.title: ISBN
fields.description: The ISBN field provides the 13-digit International Standard Book Number for all items which carry an ISBN.  [Required for books]

fields.name: EAN
fields.title: EAN
fields.description: The EAN is 13-digit International Standard Book Number + the additional 5 characters in price.  [Required for books]

fields.name: FullTitle
fields.title: FullTitle
fields.description: The Full Title field encompasses the full title of an item. This field may contain relevant information such as series number and  variant information, where applicable.  [Always required]

fields.name: PriceUSD
fields.title: PriceUSD
fields.description: The PriceUSD field includes the suggested retail price in US dollars that is printed on the product. Note that this field will not include a dollar sign ($).  Also known as the MSRP.  [Always required]

fields.name: PriceCAD
fields.title: PriceCAD
fields.description: The PriceCAD field includes the suggested retail price  in Canadian dollars. Note that this field will not include a dollar sign ($).  [Optional]

fields.name: PriceUK
fields.title: PriceUK
fields.description: The PriceUK field includes the suggested retail price in British Pound Sterling. Note that this field will not include a currency symbol.  [Optional]

fields.name: NETPrice
fields.title: NETPrice
fields.description: If an Item is flagged as NET Priced, it defaults to the USD item price. Use this field to override that default. (Example: 5.06 for an item that is net priced at $5.06)  [Optional]

fields.name: NETPriced
fields.title: NETPriced
fields.description: Indicates a product is NET priced  [Optional]

fields.name: SeriesName
fields.title: SeriesName
fields.description: The SeriesName field is the main title of the item.  [Required for comics & graphic novels]

fields.name: SeriesNumber
fields.title: SeriesNumber
fields.description: The SeriesNumber field displays the issue or volume number of the item, if it is part of a numbered series.  [Required for comics]

fields.name: SubTitle
fields.title: SubTitle
fields.description: The Subtitle field is a secondary or explanatory title, often printed on the cover of an item.  [Optional]

fields.name: CoverDescription
fields.title: CoverDescription
fields.description: The CoverDescription field describes the cover differences between different editions. Should not contain the word "Cover".  [Required for variant (non-main) covers]

fields.name: PrimaryFormatType
fields.title: PrimaryFormatType
fields.description: The PrimaryFormatType field contains a code that corresponds to the specific format of the item. Book, Merchandise (See FormatTypes in the data definitions). This should start from the outside of the product to the inside (a slipcase is outside of hardcovers, for example). For example, if an item is a box set of hardcovers, the FormatType should be BX.  [Always required]

fields.name: SecondaryFormatTypes
fields.title: SecondaryFormatTypes
fields.description: SecondaryFormatTypes further defines format types. For example, Hardcover > Omnibus (See FormatTypes in the data definitions). This field is a comma-separated list of format codes.  [Optional]

fields.name: ProductCategory
fields.title: ProductCategory
fields.description: The ProductCategory is the categorization that the product falls under for taxation purposes.  [Always required]

fields.name: TextNumber
fields.title: TextNumber
fields.description: The TextNumber field (Red, Black, Omega, etc)  [Optional]

fields.name: VolumeTag
fields.title: VolumeTag
fields.description: Is the collection referred to as a Book, Season, Volume or other?  [Optional]

fields.name: Description
fields.title: Description
fields.description: The Description field displays the full description associated with the item. Note that this text will not contain any HTML formatting, and double quotes will be escaped to maintain file integrity.  [Always required]

fields.name: InitialOrderDate
fields.title: InitialOrderDate
fields.description: Identifies when the initial orders are due on a product (YYYY-MM-DD)  [Optional]

fields.name: FOCDate
fields.title: FOCDate
fields.description: The FOCDate field provides the Final Order Cutoff date for the item, expressed in YYYY-MM-DD. Example 2023-10-13 (may be different than publisher FOC)  [Optional]

fields.name: OnSaleDate
fields.title: OnSaleDate
fields.description: The OnSaleDate field provides the expected On Sale Date/In-Store Date for the item, expressed in YYYY-MM-DD.  [Always required]

fields.name: OriginalCatalogDate
fields.title: OriginalCatalogDate
fields.description: The OriginalCatalogDate field corresponds to the date when the item was originally listed, expressed in YYYY-MM-DD. (for Offered again and Resolicited Items)  [Optional]

fields.name: PublisherName
fields.title: PublisherName
fields.description: The PublisherName field displays the name of the publisher or division that is publishing the item.  [Always required]

fields.name: ImprintName
fields.title: ImprintName
fields.description: The ImprintName field displays the name of the imprint or brand that is publishing the item. Example: DC Universe versus Black Label, Skybound for Image Comics  [Optional]

fields.name: Storyline
fields.title: Storyline
fields.description: A group of issues that tie together into one storyline, these may cross series codes, or be contained within one series code.  [Optional]

fields.name: SeriesFrequency
fields.title: SeriesFrequency
fields.description: The SeriesFrequency field discloses information specific to the publication cadence or type of the item.  [Required for comics & graphic novels]

fields.name: MaxIssue
fields.title: MaxIssue
fields.description: The Max Issue information refers to the highest number (last issue) in a limited series. For example, in Umbrella Academy: Apocalypse Suite #4 (of 6), the Max Issue data [(of 6)] identifies the title as a six-issue limited series.  [Optional]

fields.name: VariantNumber
fields.title: VariantNumber
fields.description: A numerical representation of the order in which a variant was added (i.e. the fourth added variant would have the number 4), should correspond with the last 2 or 3 digits of a UPC (depending on your ICP pattern) where possible.  [Always required]

fields.name: VariantRatio
fields.title: VariantRatio
fields.description: The VariantRatio field is a numeric field that denotes the ordering requirement, if any, to purchase the item.  This should be expressed in the format of X:Y for a 1 in 25, we would express X = 1 and Y = 25 for a result of 1:25.  If empty, we assume this product is not a ratio variant  [Optional]

fields.name: SeriesCode
fields.title: SeriesCode
fields.description: SeriesCode is used to denote a specific title numerically and encompasses all issues of a given title.  For Comics, a series code should be unique to the first 11 digits of a UPC code (with some exceptions).  [Required for comics]

fields.name: SeriesType
fields.title: SeriesType
fields.description: What type of a comic is this, for example a one-shot vs ongoing vs limited series?  [Required for comics]

fields.name: ParentSeriesCode
fields.title: ParentSeriesCode
fields.description: The ParentSeriesCode will indicate a series code that this is a child of (example: Annual is the child of a series code).  Batman (1940) is the parent of Batman New52 (2011), which is the parent of Batman Rebirth (2016).  Is only intended for the first issue of a series, or for a one-shot  [Optional]

fields.name: SeriesFamily
fields.title: SeriesFamily
fields.description: SeriesFamily represents a family of titles (All Spider-Man, All Batman, All X-Men, All Titans)  [Optional]

fields.name: PageCount
fields.title: PageCount
fields.description: The PageCount field displays the page count of the publication.  [Always required]

fields.name: PrimaryContentType
fields.title: PrimaryContentType
fields.description: PrimaryContentType defines something as primarily original content, or primarily reprinted content.  [Always required]

fields.name: IssueSort
fields.title: IssueSort
fields.description: Sorting number (which should usually be the same as the Series Number, except for when it is not. i.e. Deathmate Black, Deathmate Series, Zero Hour #4 (which was the first issue).  [Optional]

fields.name: BISAC1
fields.title: BISAC1
fields.description: The BISAC field denotes the first 9-character alphanumeric BISAC code associated with the item. The Book Industry  Standards and Communications (BISAC) Subject Heading list is an industry-approved list of subject descriptors, intended  to help retailers and data providers standardize genre and subject classification.  (NOTE: You may use any BISAC categories available from the BISG)  [Always required]

fields.name: BISAC1Description
fields.title: BISAC1Description
fields.description: The BISAC Description field provides the descriptive translation of the corresponding BISAC1 subject code.  [Always required]

fields.name: BISAC2
fields.title: BISAC2
fields.description: The BISAC field denotes the first 9-character alphanumeric BISAC code associated with the item. The Book Industry  Standards and Communications (BISAC) Subject Heading list is an industry-approved list of subject descriptors, intended  to help retailers and data providers standardize genre and subject classification.  [Optional]

fields.name: BISAC2Description
fields.title: BISAC2Description
fields.description: The BISAC Description field provides the descriptive translation of the corresponding BISAC1 subject code.  [Optional]

fields.name: BISAC3
fields.title: BISAC3
fields.description: The BISAC field denotes the first 9-character alphanumeric BISAC code associated with the item. The Book Industry  Standards and Communications (BISAC) Subject Heading list is an industry-approved list of subject descriptors, intended  to help retailers and data providers standardize genre and subject classification.  [Optional]

fields.name: BISAC3Description
fields.title: BISAC3Description
fields.description: The BISAC Description field provides the descriptive translation of the corresponding BISAC1 subject code.  [Optional]

fields.name: Language
fields.title: Language
fields.description: The Language field provides a 3-character code that provides information about the language of the item. See Language Codes  [Always required]

fields.name: AgeRange
fields.title: AgeRange
fields.description: The AgeRange field is a four-character alphanumeric range of ages that apply to the target audience of the item. See AgeRange List of Values  [Required for kids' items]

fields.name: MediaRating
fields.title: MediaRating
fields.description: The MediaRating field discloses the actual media rating value for the item. See MediaRating List of Values  [Always required]

fields.name: Adult
fields.title: Adult
fields.description: Adult (A) titles are intended for sale only to adult customers. This field identifies items which have explicit content intended for adults.  [Optional]

fields.name: VariantMechanic
fields.title: VariantMechanic
fields.description: If this is not an open order variant, the constraint type for ordering is described here.  [Optional]

fields.name: AlternateUPC
fields.title: AlternateUPC
fields.description: In the case a title has more variant covers than can be accounted for with the item's base barcode, this alternate UPC code would indicate the other barcodes used to qualify for a ratioed variant.  [Optional]

fields.name: OrderRequirementCodes
fields.title: OrderRequirementCodes
fields.description: OrderRequirementCodes is a comma separated list containing barcodes that may be used in conjunction with ExceedPercentage or ratio variants. Ratio and non-ratio variants, by default count toward these thresholds unless listed in the OrderExceptionCodes.  [Optional]

fields.name: OrderExceptionCodes
fields.title: OrderExceptionCodes
fields.description: Comma separated list of barcodes outside of the default set that are to be EXCLUDED in ratio threshhold math.  [Optional]

fields.name: ExceedPercentage
fields.title: ExceedPercentage
fields.description: X% of the item identified in the OrderRequirementCodes field.  [Optional]

fields.name: EligibleCodes
fields.title: EligibleCodes
fields.description: The EligibleCodes field is a numeric field of barcodes that should be used in conjunction with ExceedPercentage. Barcodes in this field will be used to determine the total quantity of all items of a specific title that must meet or exceed the quantity calculated by ExceedPercentage  [Optional]

fields.name: PublisherCode
fields.title: PublisherCode
fields.description: Numerical representation of the 5 digit "Manufacturer Code" used to designate a UPC code  [Always required]

fields.name: ContentItems
fields.title: ContentItems
fields.description: Text field that indicates what is in the collection.  [Optional]

fields.name: Reprint
fields.title: Reprint
fields.description: Is the item a Reprint? Facsimile editions - Image Firsts, something that has come out previously.  [Required for comics]

fields.name: Printing
fields.title: Printing
fields.description: What printing is the item  [Always required]

fields.name: WriterISNI
fields.title: WriterISNI
fields.description: The ISNI number for the Writer, comma separated list of all writers on the item, multiple creators should be separated by a semicolon, this field should be text.  [Optional]

fields.name: Writer
fields.title: Writer
fields.description: The Writer field identifies the primary creator responsible for authoring/writing the item. If there is more than one  contributor per role, all contributors will be displayed as FIRST LAST name and separated by a semicolon.  [Always required]

fields.name: ArtistISNI
fields.title: ArtistISNI
fields.description: The ISNI number for the Artist, comma separated list of all artists on the item, multiple creators should be separated by a semicolon, this field should be text.  [Optional]

fields.name: Artist
fields.title: Artist
fields.description: The Arist field identifies the primary creator responsible for illustrating the item. If there is more than one  contributor per role, all contributors will be displayed as FIRST LAST name and separated by a semicolon.  [Required for comics]

fields.name: CoverArtistISNI
fields.title: CoverArtistISNI
fields.description: The ISNI number for the Cover Artist, comma separated list of all artists on the item, multiple creators should be separated by a semicolon, this field should be text.  [Optional]

fields.name: CoverArtist
fields.title: CoverArtist
fields.description: The CoverArtist field identifies the primary creator responsible for illustrating the cover of the item. If there is more than one  contributor per role, all contributors will be displayed as FIRST LAST name and separated by a semicolon.  [Required for comics]

fields.name: ColoristISNI
fields.title: ColoristISNI
fields.description: The ISNI number for the Colorist (if any), comma separated list of all colorists on the item, multiple creators should be separated by a semicolon, this field should be text.  [Optional]

fields.name: Colorist
fields.title: Colorist
fields.description: The Colorist field identifies the primary creator responsible for coloring the item. If there is more than one  contributor per role, all contributors will be displayed as FIRST LAST name and separated by a semicolon.  [Optional]

fields.name: InkerISNI
fields.title: InkerISNI
fields.description: The ISNI number for the Inker (if any), comma separated list of all inkers on the item, multiple creators should be separated by a semicolon, this field should be text.  [Optional]

fields.name: Inker
fields.title: Inker
fields.description: The Inker field identifies the primary creator responsible for inking the item. If there is more than one  contributor per role, all contributors will be displayed as FIRST LAST name and separated by a semicolon.  [Optional]

fields.name: EditorISNI
fields.title: EditorISNI
fields.description: The ISNI number for the Editor (if any), comma separated list of all editors on the item, multiple creators should be separated by a semicolon, this field should be text.  [Optional]

fields.name: Editor
fields.title: Editor
fields.description: The Editor field identifies the primary creator responsible for editing the item. If there is more than one  contributor per role, all contributors will be displayed as FIRST LAST name and separated by a semicolon.  [Optional]

fields.name: LettererISNI
fields.title: LettererISNI
fields.description: The ISNI number for the Letterer (if any), comma separated list of all letterers on the item, multiple creators should be separated by a semicolon, this field should be text.  [Optional]

fields.name: Letterer
fields.title: Letterer
fields.description: The Letterer field identifies the primary creator responsible for lettering the item. If there is more than one  contributor per role, all contributors will be displayed as FIRST LAST name and separated by a semicolon.  [Optional]

fields.name: Contributor
fields.title: Contributor
fields.description: The Contributor field identifies the any other primary creator responsible for some other contribution to the item. If there is more than one  contributor per role, all contributors will be displayed as FIRST LAST name and separated by a semicolon.  [Optional]

fields.name: CartonQuantity
fields.title: CartonQuantity
fields.description: The CartonQuantity field displays the number of individual units contained in a single carton.  [Optional]

fields.name: UnitMeasurements
fields.title: UnitMeasurements
fields.description: Imperial or Metric  [Always required]

fields.name: Unit Width
fields.title: UnitWidth
fields.description: The Width field displays the width of the item in inches (Imperial), or cm (Metric), rounded to the fourth decimal place.  [Always required]

fields.name: Unit Height
fields.title: UnitHeight
fields.description: The Height field displays the  height of the item in inches (Imperial), or cm (Metric), rounded to the fourth decimal place.  [Always required]

fields.name: Unit Depth
fields.title: UnitDepth
fields.description: The depth field displays the depth of the item in inches (Imperial), or cm (Metric), rounded to the fourth decimal place.  [Always required]

fields.name: Unit Weight
fields.title: UnitWeight
fields.description: The weight field displays the weight of the item in pounds (Imperial) or grams (Metric), rounded to the fourth decimal place.  [Always required]

fields.name: Case Weight
fields.title: CaseWeight
fields.description: The weight of a case pounds (Imperial) or grams (Metric), rounded to the fourth decimal place.  [Optional]

fields.name: Case Length
fields.title: CaseLength
fields.description: The length of a case inches (Imperial), or cm (Metric), rounded to the fourth decimal place.  [Optional]

fields.name: Case Width
fields.title: CaseWidth
fields.description: The width of a case inches (Imperial), or cm (Metric), rounded to the fourth decimal place.  [Optional]

fields.name: Case Height
fields.title: CaseHeight
fields.description: The height of the case inches (Imperial), or cm (Metric), rounded to the fourth decimal place.  [Optional]

fields.name: StockCode
fields.title: StockCode
fields.description: The Primary ID of the item that can be tracked month-to-month  [Always required]

fields.name: MonthCode
fields.title: MonthCode
fields.description: The ID of the item for a specific catalogue month (traditionally MMMYY#### format)  [Optional]

fields.name: AlternateCode
fields.title: AlternateCode
fields.description: Any alternate codes for other distribution arms.  [Optional]

fields.name: OfferedAgain
fields.title: OfferedAgain
fields.description: Sometimes, items are solicited more than once in subsequent catalogues. This field denotes if an item is being Offered Again.  [Optional]

fields.name: Resolicited
fields.title: Resolicited
fields.description: A re-issued item was previously listed, but was cancelled before shipping and is being listed again. No previous orders were committed, and this re-issue will be the first time this product is being ordered.  [Optional]

fields.name: FOCItem
fields.title: FOCItem
fields.description: This field denotes whether a title is eligible for order adjustments by the Final Order Cutoff.  [Optional]

fields.name: DemandCheck
fields.title: DemandCheck
fields.description: After the Final Order Cut-off deadline, the publisher will evaluate the orders and determine if the product will be reprinted. If the orders do not justify a reprint, all orders will be canceled, and retailers will be notified via the Order Cancellation section of their invoices.  [Optional]

fields.name: InitialOrderIncrement
fields.title: InitialOrderIncrement
fields.description: Minimum quantity required to order, similar to a case quantity.  [Optional]

fields.name: ReOrderIncrement
fields.title: ReOrderIncrement
fields.description: Minimum quantity required to re-order, similar to a case quantity.  [Optional]

fields.name: BundleQuantity
fields.title: BundleQuantity
fields.description: Quantity of breakable units in a bundle.  [Optional]

fields.name: BundleUPCs
fields.title: BundleUPCs
fields.description: What UPCs are within this bundle of items?  [Optional]

fields.name: SalesRestrictions
fields.title: SalesRestrictions
fields.description: The SalesRestrictions field displays the geographic territories where the item may not be sold, in a comma separated list. See list of country codes (based on 2 character ISO 3166 country codes).  [Optional]

fields.name: OrderFormNotes
fields.title: OrderFormNotes
fields.description: Order Form Notes are parenthetical descriptions, added below catalogue line items, offering additional ordering information on solicited merchandise.  [Optional]

fields.name: CatalogPage
fields.title: CatalogPage
fields.description: This field identifies the page number in the distributor catalogue on which an item’s solicitation information can be found.  [Optional]

fields.name: CatalogMonth
fields.title: CatalogMonth
fields.description: Identifies the original date the item was solicited.  Also known as "Catalogue Date". (YYYY-MM), for items that are cancelled and re-solicited, this date will be for the NEW solicitation date.  [Optional]

fields.name: DiscountCode
fields.title: DiscountCode
fields.description: Discount codes are the code which designates which discount an account is eligible for. This supports fluctuating discounts based on publisher or vendor, and requires a table to decode.  [Optional]

fields.name: ReorderDiscountCode
fields.title: ReorderDiscountCode
fields.description: Discount codes are the code which designates which discount an account is eligible for a reorder.  [Optional]

fields.name: Returnable
fields.title: Returnable
fields.description: The item is returnable  [Optional]

fields.name: ReturnWindow
fields.title: ReturnWindow
fields.description: The ReturnWindow field corresponds to the applicable date when an item can be returned  YYYY-MM-DD. Example 2021-05-01.  [Optional]

fields.name: ReturnType
fields.title: ReturnType
fields.description: The type of returnability (affidavit, stripped cover, return to publisher, etc)  [Optional]

fields.name: ReturnQualifier
fields.title: ReturnQualifier
fields.description: Open return, vs returns with qualifiers.  [Optional]

fields.name: ReturnPercent
fields.title: ReturnPercent
fields.description: Percentage of FOC order you may return  [Optional]

fields.name: ImageUrl
fields.title: ImageUrl
fields.description: The ImageURL field is a persistent URL that points to a high-resolution cover image for the item. If the field is blank, it  means that a cover image is not yet available.  [Optional]

fields.name: ImageUpdateDate
fields.title: ImageUpdateDate
fields.description: The date the image was last updated. Expressed in YYYY-MM-DD. Example, 2021-05-01. Should be populated with the date the first time when the ImageURL has a value.  [Optional]

fields.name: VariantType
fields.title: VariantType
fields.description: This indicates whether something is a primary or variant cover.  Primary being the first (or main_ cover in a product.  Mandatory for comics only, optional for Graphic Novels.  [Required for comics]

fields.name: SalesStatusCode
fields.title: SalesStatusCode
fields.description: A two digit code that indicates the status of a product. (See Sales Status Codes in Data Definitions)  [Optional]

fields.name: SalesTerritory
fields.title: SalesTerritory
fields.description: Positive list of territories an item is available in.  [Optional]

fields.name: MaxOrderQuantity
fields.title: MaxOrderQuantity
fields.description: The MaxOrderQuantity field indicates the maximum orderable quantity, if any, of that item. If blank, there is no limit.  [Optional]

fields.name: CoverColor
fields.title: CoverColor
fields.description: This field is a simple indicator if a cover does or does not have color.  [Optional]

fields.name: Translator
fields.title: Translator
fields.description: Translator of the material.  [Optional]

fields.name: CreatorBio
fields.title: CreatorBio
fields.description: Bio of Creators  [Optional]

fields.name: Reviews
fields.title: Reviews
fields.description: Reviews or Pull Quotes for the item  [Optional]

fields.name: ComparableTitles
fields.title: ComparableTitles
fields.description: Other "Similar" titles, as a comma separated UPC/ISBN list.  [Optional]

fields.name: NETPriceCA
fields.title: NETPriceCA
fields.description: NET Price in CAD  [Optional]

fields.name: NETPricedCA
fields.title: NETPricedCA
fields.description: Boolean Y/N to indicate there is a Net Price in CAD  [Optional]

fields.name: NETPriceUK
fields.title: NETPriceUK
fields.description: NET Price in UK  [Optional]

fields.name: NETPricedUK
fields.title: NETPricedUK
fields.description: Boolean Y/N to indicate there is a Net Price in UK  [Optional]

fields.name: DistributorPublisherCode
fields.title: DistributorPublisherCode
fields.description: A code for the publisher that indicates the internal code for the distributor.  [Optional]

fields.name: TitleFamilyID
fields.title: TitleFamilyID
fields.description: An ID to provide a linking between all formats and variants of a given title, comic, tpb, graphic novel, hardcover, deluxe hardcover, etc.  [Optional]

fields.name: HSCode
fields.title: HSCode
fields.description: HS Codes are a six digit code that is an internationally recognized standard.  [Optional]

fields.name: CountryOfOrigin
fields.title: CountryOfOrigin
fields.description: 4 digit code (3 digits and a suffex) that determines the country of origin.  [Optional]

fields.name: OtherMarketOnSaleDate
fields.title: OtherMarketOnSaleDate
fields.description: The date that other markets outside the comics industry may offer this item on sale, expressed in YYYY-MM-DD. Example 2023-10-13  [Optional]

fields.name: DTCStreetDate
fields.title: DTCStreetDate
fields.description: The date a direct to consumer publisher may offer this item on sale, expressed in YYYY-MM-DD. Example 2023-10-13  [Optional]
